fish.note: Work the jetty rocks on the push; let it sink into the wash.
Drag the channel edge slow on the outgoing; expect a soft tap then weight.
First trough on a building high tide at the sandy corner of the jetty.
Chase boils at the harbor mouth on a grey-light morning; fast iron retrieve.
Sandy mud flat adjacent to the channel on the drop; slow yo-yo retrieve.
Rocky base of the west jetty on a building tide; sensitive bottom rig.
Deep channel edge at first light in spring; ghost shrimp or live squid.
Read the troughs at low; fish the foam seam on the push.
Sight-fish them tailing in inches of water on a warm incoming flood.
First trough along the sandy stretch; sand crab after dark.
Shares the trough with spotfin; mussel and ghost shrimp both work.
Sandy flat between the jetties on the outgoing; swimbait dragged slow.
Tuck casts tight to the rock fingers at the south end of the beach.
Stay tight to structure; pull hard early or they bury you in the kelp.
Reef edges and crevices at 20–40 ft; let bait sit on the bottom.
Berley a wash zone over rock with frozen peas; small hooks, light leader.
Sandy gaps between reef sections on the drop; slow yo-yo retrieve.
Sits motionless on the reef bottom; drop bait straight down over structure.
Mid-water over the kelp canopy; bait or small jig works well.
Kelp edges at first light in spring and summer; live squid or mackerel.
Dock pilings and eelgrass edges; reaction bites on the drop.
Fan-cast the harbor sand flats off the launch ramp.
Load the bait tank off the pier at first light with a sabiki.
Rocky entrance boulders and kelp on the outside of the jetty.
Harbor mouth on a grey-light morning when schools push inside.
Inside the harbor near sandy patches after dark; mussel or shrimp.
Anywhere in the harbor at night; easy to catch, great live bait.
Points and timber early; red-clay cliffs at 18–25 ft midday. Catch-and-release.
Soak cut bait on the west shore flats or near the dam after dark.
Suspend a mini-jig over brush piles; floating lantern after dark.
Stocked regularly Nov–Apr; troll a gold Kastmaster or soak PowerBait near inlet.
Dock edges and reed beds with crickets or waxworms; great fun on light tackle.
Deep structure near the dam on swimbaits or live crawfish at first and last light.
